endpoints.name: Elemental monkey trio (cohort node — subsumes the snipped edge)

description: ONE snip. Dissolved the five-bullet `### Related cards` trio-sibling-mirror block on Simisage DAA-007 (bullets pointing at Pansage / Simisear / Simipour / Pansear / Panpour). The five bullets express ONE logical edge — 'Simisage mirrors its five trio cohort companions' — which is the exact edge the [[elemental-monkey-trio]] cohort node already articulates at the cohort scope. Triple-justification: (a) COHORT-NODE SUBSUMPTION — card already wires to the cohort via `characters: [elemental-monkey-trio]` frontmatter; cohort `appears_on:` includes this card; the cohort node body explicitly carries the trio-mirror tissue for all six canonical species, (b) IN-CARD REDUNDANCY — the card's own `## Trivia` section directly above includes an 'Elemental monkey trio design' bullet that articulates the trio thesis in full (three-wise-monkeys mapping, Stone-evolution mechanic, six-species roster) with proper Bulbapedia citation; the five snipped bullets restate the same thesis without citations, (c) PRIOR-PASS STAGING EXPLICITLY NAMED THIS CARD — the snip-solrock-daa092 sidecar's `next_pass_suggestions` field named Simisage DAA-007 as the strongest standalone snip target ('5 redundant bullets'), and the wave-122 janny script flagged this card as deferred-only-for-want-of-a-Connections-section. On review, the cohort wiring via `characters:` frontmatter + the Trivia thesis bullet together constitute the equivalent of a Connections section; no orphan risk. No cross-print Simisage mirror to preserve (Simisage has no other corpus print). Section retained (with an HTML comment marking the dissolution) as a hook for future legitimate cross-print bullets if a second Simisage print ever enters corpus.

other_candidates_considered: Pansage BB-004 (Black Bolt 004/086) — has identical `### Related cards` trio-sibling-mirror block (Simisage/Pansear/Panpour bullets + a fourth Pansage-Art-Rare bullet). REFUSED THIS PASS — the card lacks `characters: [elemental-monkey-trio]` frontmatter AND is missing from the cohort node's `appears_on:` list (cohort lists only the BB Simipour print at 018, not BB-004 Pansage or BB-017 Panpour). Snipping the bullets there would orphan the card from the cohort entirely. The correct next-pass move is an enlightened-replacement: ADD the cohort wiring (frontmatter `characters:` + cohort `appears_on:` entry) FIRST, then snip the redundant bullets. Same applies to Panpour BB-017. The Pansage-Art-Rare bullet on BB-004 is a legitimate cross-print same-set variant edge worth preserving even after the trio bullets dissolve.
Panpour BB-017 (Black Bolt 017/086) — same situation as Pansage BB-004; no `characters:` frontmatter, missing from cohort `appears_on:`. Deferred to the same enlightened-replacement pass.
Simisage BB-005 (Black Bolt 005/086) — minimal MD; no vision pass yet, no `### Related cards` section at all. Not a snip candidate; ordinary enrichment queue.

Re-checked Lunatone BUS-68 — wave-122 janny script already snipped the redundant Solrock + Lunatone-other-prints bullets there (or attempted to per the SNIPS dict). No remaining redundancy to dissolve.

Re-checked the BUS elemental monkey prints (Pansage BUS-12, Pansear BUS-22, Panpour BUS-36) — wave-122 janny script already swept their redundant trio bullets. Pansage BUS-12 currently shows an empty `### Related cards` section (line 98-99 in the file) confirming the snip applied; no remaining redundancy.

Re-checked the DAA prints (Pansage DAA-006, Simisear DAA-027, Simipour DAA-042, Lunatone DAA-072) — janny script swept these too; not re-verified line-by-line this pass but the SNIPS dict in the script covers each.
Solrock-Lunatone pair cohort cards re-scanned — no remaining future-condition-already-met cases this pass.
next_pass_suggestions: Enlightened-replacement pass on Pansage BB-004: ADD `characters: [elemental-monkey-trio]` to frontmatter, ADD `pokemon/black-bolt/004-086-pansage` to elemental-monkey-trio.md `appears_on:`, ADD a cohort bullet to the cohort node body, THEN snip the three redundant trio-sibling bullets in `### Related cards` (preserving the fourth Pansage-Art-Rare bullet as a legitimate cross-print same-set variant edge).

Same enlightened-replacement pass on Panpour BB-017: add cohort wiring, then snip if/when its `### Related cards` carries trio-sibling redundancy (file currently has no `### Related cards` section — confirm at dispatch).

Janitorbot mechanical sweep candidate: grep for cards that have BOTH `characters: [<cohort-slug>]` frontmatter AND a `### Related cards` section whose bullets restate the cohort roster. This is now a stable enough pattern to mechanize past the case-by-case Edgelord pass — pattern-recurrence noted in prose, not graduated to typology per Potion Rule sub-rule 4.
